info.name: Microsoft Azure Azure Quota Extension API

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Scope Providers Microsoft Quota Usages Resourcename

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.

variable.description: (Required) The target Azure resource URI. For example, `/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/qms-test/providers/Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts/testAccount/`. This is the target Azure resource URI for the List GET operation. If a `{resourceName}` is added after `/quotas`, then it's the target Azure resource URI in the GET operation for the specific resource.

(Required) Resource name for a given resource provider. For example:
- SKU name for Microsoft.Compute
- SKU or TotalLowPriorityCores for Microsoft.MachineLearningServices
 For Microsoft.Network PublicIPAddresses.

request.description: Get the current usage of a resource.

response.name: OK. The usage information will be returned in the response.

request.description: Get the quota limit of a resource. The response can be used to determine the remaining quota to calculate a new quota limit that can be submitted with a PUT request.

response.name: OK. The quota information will be returned in the response with pagination.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Put Scope Providers Microsoft Quota Quotas Resourcename

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.

variable.description: (Required) The target Azure resource URI. For example, `/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/qms-test/providers/Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts/testAccount/`. This is the target Azure resource URI for the List GET operation. If a `{resourceName}` is added after `/quotas`, then it's the target Azure resource URI in the GET operation for the specific resource.

(Required) Resource name for a given resource provider. For example:
- SKU name for Microsoft.Compute
- SKU or TotalLowPriorityCores for Microsoft.MachineLearningServices
 For Microsoft.Network PublicIPAddresses.

request.description:
Create or update the quota limit for the specified resource with the requested value. To update the quota, follow these steps:
1. Use the GET operation for quotas and usages to determine how much quota remains for the specific resource and to calculate the new quota limit. These steps are detailed in [this example](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-governance-and-management/using-the-new-quota-rest-api/ba-p/2183670).
2. Use this PUT operation to update the quota limit. Please check the URI in location header for the detailed status of the request.

response.name: OK. Returns the quota request details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.

response.name: The request is accepted and is being processed. To check the status for this request, use the value for the quota request ID **id** obtained in the response of a [Quota Request Status](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/reserved-vm-instances/quotar

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Patch Scope Providers Microsoft Quota Quotas Resourcename

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.

variable.description: (Required) The target Azure resource URI. For example, `/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/qms-test/providers/Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts/testAccount/`. This is the target Azure resource URI for the List GET operation. If a `{resourceName}` is added after `/quotas`, then it's the target Azure resource URI in the GET operation for the specific resource.

(Required) Resource name for a given resource provider. For example:
- SKU name for Microsoft.Compute
- SKU or TotalLowPriorityCores for Microsoft.MachineLearningServices
 For Microsoft.Network PublicIPAddresses.

request.description:
Update the quota limit for a specific resource to the specified value:
1. Use the Usages-GET and Quota-GET operations to determine the remaining quota for the specific resource and to calculate the new quota limit. These steps are detailed in [this example](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-governance-and-management/using-the-new-quota-rest-api/ba-p/2183670).
2. Use this PUT operation to update the quota limit. Please check the URI in location header for the detailed status of the request.

response.name: OK. Returns the quota request details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.

response.name: The request is accepted and is being processed. To check the status for this request, use the value for the quota request ID **id** in a [Quota Request Status](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/reserved-vm-instances/quotarequeststatus/get) GET ope

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Scope Providers Microsoft Quota Quotarequests

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.
Number of records to return.

The **Skiptoken** parameter is used only if a previous operation returned a partial result. If a previous response contains a **nextLink** element, its value includes a **skiptoken** parameter that specifies a starting point to use for subsequent calls.

variable.description: (Required) The target Azure resource URI. For example, `/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/qms-test/providers/Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts/testAccount/`. This is the target Azure resource URI for the List GET operation. If a `{resourceName}` is added after `/quotas`, then it's the target Azure resource URI in the GET operation for the specific resource.

request.description: For the specified scope, get the current quota requests for a one year period ending at the time is made. Use the **oData** filter to select quota requests.

response.name: OK. Sending quota request status and details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for this operation.
Number of records to return.

The **Skiptoken** parameter is used only if a previous operation returned a partial result. If a previous response contains a **nextLink** element, its value includes a **skiptoken** parameter that specifies a starting point to use for subsequent calls.
